pages.title: Atlassian
pages.content:
# Atlassian

> Atlassian is a global enterprise software company that builds tools for software development, project management, collaboration, and IT service management. Founded in 2002 in Sydney, Australia, Atlassian serves over 300,000 customers worldwide — from startups to Fortune 500 enterprises — helping teams plan, track, collaborate, and ship work together.

Atlassian's product portfolio spans the full software development and business collaboration lifecycle, organized into three collections: Teamwork (planning & collaboration), Service (ITSM & customer support), and Strategy (goals & portfolio management). Together these form the Atlassian System of Work — an integrated platform that connects goals to execution across every team. Products are available as cloud-hosted (Atlassian Cloud) or self-managed (Data Center) deployments. Atlassian Intelligence, powered by AI, is embedded across the platform to help teams work smarter. Atlassian also provides official MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for AI agent integration.

Key terms: Project Management, Issue Tracking, Agile, Scrum, Kanban, ITSM, IT Service Management, Knowledge Management, Wiki, DevOps, CI/CD, Git, Source Code Management, Collaboration, Teamwork, AI, Atlassian Intelligence, Rovo, Rovo Dev, Rovo Studio, MCP, Model Context Protocol, Agents, Teamwork Graph, Cloud, Data Center, Marketplace, Forge, Connect, Automation, Workflows, System of Work, Teamwork Collection, Service Collection, Strategy Collection, DX, Dia

## Atlassian System of Work

- [Atlassian System of Work](https://www.atlassian.com/system-of-work): Atlassian's integrated platform that connects strategy, planning, tracking, collaboration, and service delivery across every team — from executives to engineers. Replaces siloed tools with a unified system.
- [Teamwork Collection](https://www.atlassian.com/collections/teamwork): The Teamwork Collection bundles Jira, Confluence, Loom, and Focus for cross-functional team collaboration — planning, tracking, documentation, and async communication in one connected experience.
- [Service Collection](https://www.atlassian.com/collections/service): The Service Collection bundles Jira Service Management, Statuspage, Opsgenie, and Compass for end-to-end IT service management, incident response, and operational health — from request to resolution.
- [Strategy Collection](https://www.atlassian.com/collections/strategy): The Strategy Collection includes Jira Align and Focus for connecting business strategy to technical execution — OKRs, portfolio management, and cross-team goal tracking at enterprise scale.
- [Product Collection](https://www.atlassian.com/collections/product): The Product Collection helps product teams capture feedback at scale, uncover insights, and prioritize ideas so they can build with confidence.

## Products

- [Jira](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira): The #1 project and issue tracking tool for agile software development teams. Plan, track, and release software with Scrum boards, Kanban boards, roadmaps, and reporting. Supports agile methodologies, bug tracking, and project planning for teams of all sizes.
- [Confluence](https://www.atlassian.com/software/confluence): Team workspace and wiki for creating, organizing, and collaborating on documentation, project plans, meeting notes, and knowledge bases. Features real-time editing, page trees, templates, and powerful search.
- [Jira Service Management](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/service-management): IT service management (ITSM) and customer service platform. Provides service desk, incident management, problem management, change management, asset management, and SLA tracking. ITIL-certified.
- [Bitbucket](https://www.atlassian.com/software/bitbucket): Git-based source code management and CI/CD platform. Supports code review via pull requests, branch permissions, pipelines for automated builds, tests, and deployments. Available as Cloud and Data Center.
- [Trello](https://www.atlassian.com/software/trello): Visual project management tool using boards, lists, and cards. Ideal for lightweight task management, team coordination, and personal productivity. Features Power-Ups for extended functionality.
- [Loom](https://www.loom.com/): Async video messaging platform for work communication. Record your screen and camera to create quick video messages, tutorials, and walkthroughs. Features AI-powered summaries, transcriptions, and engagement analytics.
- [Jira Product Discovery](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/product-discovery): Product management and prioritization tool. Helps product teams capture ideas, prioritize features using impact scoring, and connect discovery to delivery in Jira.
- [Focus](https://www.atlassian.com/software/atlas): Teamwork directory (formerly Atlas) that connects teams, goals, and projects across the organization. Provides visibility into what teams are working on, their status, and how work connects to company objectives and OKRs.
- [Compass](https://www.atlassian.com/software/compass): Developer experience platform for tracking software components, services, APIs, and libraries. Provides a software catalog, scorecards for health tracking, and integrations with CI/CD tools.
- [Statuspage](https://www.atlassian.com/software/statuspage): Incident communication platform. Create public or private status pages to communicate real-time system status, scheduled maintenance, and incident updates to users and stakeholders.
- [Opsgenie](https://www.atlassian.com/software/opsgenie): Incident management and alerting platform. Routes alerts, notifies on-call teams, and orchestrates incident response across multiple monitoring and chat tools.
- [Guard](https://www.atlassian.com/software/guard): Cloud security and organization management. Provides centralized identity management, SSO/SAML, audit logging, data security policies, and threat detection across Atlassian Cloud products.
- [Jira Align](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/align): Enterprise agile planning platform. Connects business strategy to technical execution with portfolio management, OKRs, dependency tracking, and program increment planning at scale.
- [DX (Developer Experience)](https://getdx.com/): Atlassian's developer experience platform. Measures and improves engineering team productivity, developer satisfaction, and software delivery performance with data-driven insights and actionable recommendations.
- [Dia Browser](https://www.diabrowser.com/): Atlassian's AI-native browser designed for work. Combines browsing with AI-powered assistance, context-aware search, and deep integration with Atlassian tools to reimagine how teams interact with information on the web.

## AI, Agents & Automation

- [Atlassian Intelligence](https://www.atlassian.com/platform/artificial-intelligence): AI capabilities embedded across all Atlassian products, powered by large language models and the Teamwork Graph. Features include natural language to JQL, AI-powered search, content summarization, writing assistance, automated workflows, and context-aware recommendations.
- [Rovo](https://www.atlassian.com/software/rovo): AI-powered knowledge discovery, search, and virtual agent platform. Searches across Atlassian and third-party tools to find information, answer questions, and automate tasks using AI agents. The foundation of Atlassian's AI agent ecosystem.
- [Rovo Agents](https://www.atlassian.com/software/rovo#agents): Build and deploy custom AI agents that can search, learn, and take action across your Atlassian tools and connected third-party apps. Automate repetitive tasks and create intelligent workflows.
- [Rovo Studio](https://www.atlassian.com/software/rovo/studio): Visual builder for creating, customizing, and deploying Rovo AI agents. Design agent workflows, define agent capabilities, connect knowledge sources, and publish agents to your organization without writing code.
- [Rovo Search](https://www.atlassian.com/software/rovo#search): AI-powered enterprise search across Atlassian products and 30+ third-party integrations (Google Drive, Slack, GitHub, Figma, etc.). Delivers contextual answers powered by the Teamwork Graph, not just links.
- [Rovo Dev](https://www.atlassian.com/software/rovo-dev): AI-powered coding agent for software development. Integrates with IDEs (VS Code, Cursor, JetBrains) to help developers search, plan, code, test, and take action across Atlassian tools and codebases. Uses MCP servers for deep tool integration.
- [Rovo Chat](https://www.atlassian.com/software/rovo#chat): Conversational AI assistant embedded across Atlassian products. Ask questions about your projects, get summaries, create content, and take action — all through natural language conversation grounded in your team's data.
- [Atlassian MCP Server](https://www.atlassian.com/platform/remote-mcp-server): Atlassian's official Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for AI agent integration. Provides programmatic, LLM-friendly access to Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket, and the Atlassian Design System via the open MCP standard. Compatible with Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, VS Code, and any MCP-enabled AI tool.
- [Teamwork Graph](https://www.atlassian.com/platform/teamwork-graph): Atlassian's knowledge graph that connects people, projects, goals, and content across all Atlassian products. Powers Atlassian Intelligence with organizational context so AI understands who works on what, how work connects, and what matters most.
- [Jira + AI](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/ai): AI-powered features in Jira including smart issue creation, natural language to JQL, AI-generated summaries, automated backlog grooming, sprint planning assistance, and predictive analytics for delivery estimation.
- [Jira + Agents](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/features#agents): AI agents embedded in Jira that automate project management tasks — triage incoming issues, suggest assignees, update statuses, generate reports, and proactively surface blockers and risks across your projects.
- [Confluence + AI](https://www.atlassian.com/software/confluence/ai): AI-powered features in Confluence including page summarization, writing assistance, content generation from prompts, smart recommendations, automated catch-up summaries, and AI-powered Q&A over your knowledge base.
- [Confluence + Agents](https://www.atlassian.com/software/confluence/features#agents): AI agents embedded in Confluence that help teams manage knowledge — automatically organize pages, generate documentation from templates, answer questions from page content, and keep knowledge bases accurate and up to date.
- [JSM + AI](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/service-management/features#ai): AI-powered features in Jira Service Management including virtual service agents, intelligent ticket routing, automated incident response, AI-generated knowledge articles, and predictive SLA management.
- [Automation for Jira](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/features/automation): No-code automation engine for Jira. Create rules with triggers, conditions, and actions to automate repetitive tasks, notifications, field updates, and cross-product workflows. Supports AI-powered smart automation suggestions.

## Platform & Developer Resources

- [Atlassian Developer Documentation](https://developer.atlassian.com/): Comprehensive developer documentation for building apps, integrations, and extensions on the Atlassian platform. Covers REST APIs, GraphQL, webhooks, events, and SDKs.
- [Forge](https://developer.atlassian.com/platform/forge/): Atlassian's cloud app development platform. Build and host apps directly on Atlassian infrastructure using serverless functions, UI Kit, and Custom UI. Supports Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket, and Compass.
- [Atlassian Connect](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/getting-started-with-connect/): Framework for building cloud apps that integrate with Atlassian products. Apps run on your own infrastructure and connect via REST APIs, webhooks, and iframes.
- [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/): Discover and install over 6,000 apps and integrations for Atlassian products. Categories include project management, automation, reporting, design, security, and developer tools.
- [Atlassian Design System](https://atlassian.design/): Open-source design system used across all Atlassian products. Includes components, design tokens, icons, patterns, and accessibility guidelines for building consistent Atlassian-style UIs.
- [Jira REST API](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v3/intro/): REST API for programmatic access to Jira Cloud — create and update issues, manage projects, search with JQL, manage users, and automate workflows.
- [Confluence REST API](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/confluence/rest/v2/intro/): REST API for programmatic access to Confluence Cloud — create and update pages, manage spaces, search content, and manage users.
- [Jira Service Management REST API](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/service-desk/rest/intro/): REST API for managing service requests, customers, queues, SLAs, and knowledge base articles in Jira Service Management Cloud.
- [Bitbucket REST API](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/): REST API for Bitbucket Cloud — manage repositories, pull requests, pipelines, deployments, and branch permissions.
- [Atlassian GraphQL Gateway](https://developer.atlassian.com/platform/atlassian-graphql-api/): Unified GraphQL API providing access to data across Atlassian products through a single endpoint. Query and mutate Jira, Confluence, and platform data.
- [Rovo Dev MCP Documentation](https://support.atlassian.com/rovo/docs/rovo-dev-and-model-context-protocol-mcp/): Guide for connecting AI development tools to Atlassian via MCP. Set up Rovo Dev in your IDE, configure MCP servers, and enable AI agents to read and write Jira, Confluence, and Bitbucket data.

## Pricing

- [Jira Pricing](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/pricing): Free for up to 10 users. Standard, Premium, and Enterprise tiers available. Cloud and Data Center deployment options.
- [Confluence Pricing](https://www.atlassian.com/software/confluence/pricing): Free for up to 10 users. Standard, Premium, and Enterprise tiers. Includes unlimited pages and spaces.
- [Jira Service Management Pricing](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/service-management/pricing): Free for up to 3 agents. Standard, Premium, and Enterprise tiers. Unlimited customers on all plans.
- [Bitbucket Pricing](https://www.atlassian.com/software/bitbucket/pricing): Free for up to 5 users. Standard and Premium tiers. Includes Bitbucket Pipelines CI/CD minutes.
- [Trello Pricing](https://www.atlassian.com/software/trello/pricing): Free tier with unlimited cards. Standard, Premium, and Enterprise tiers with advanced features like Timeline, Dashboard, and admin controls.
- [Loom Pricing](https://www.loom.com/pricing): Free Starter plan. Business and Enterprise tiers with advanced analytics, custom branding, and admin controls.
- [Rovo Pricing](https://www.atlassian.com/software/rovo/pricing): Pricing for Rovo AI agents, search, and chat capabilities. Includes Rovo Dev pricing for AI-assisted development.

## Deployment Options

- [Atlassian Cloud](https://www.atlassian.com/cloud): Fully managed cloud hosting by Atlassian. Automatic updates, built-in security, and global infrastructure. Available in Standard, Premium, and Enterprise tiers.
- [Atlassian Data Center](https://www.atlassian.com/enterprise/data-center): Self-managed deployment for organizations with specific compliance, performance, or data residency requirements. Supports clustering for high availability.
- [Atlassian Cloud Migration](https://www.atlassian.com/migration/cloud): Resources, tools, and guidance for migrating from Server or Data Center to Atlassian Cloud. Includes free migration trials and dedicated support.

## Manage Preferences

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. 
[More information](https://www.atlassian.com/legal/cookies )

Accept all

#### Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

#### Targeting Cookies

Always Active

These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

#### Functional Cookies

Always Active

These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.

#### Performance Cookies

Always Active

These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

##### eazyBI + Atlassian

# eazyBI's road to success

eazyBI scales their easy-to-use business intelligence application with a Marketplace app

---

##### Locations

Latvia

##### Products

[Jira Software](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira)

##### Marketplace Apps

[eazyBI app for Jira](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1211508/eazybi-reports-and-charts-for-jira-cloud?hosting=cloud&tab=overview)

##### Share Page

- 
- 
- 

eazyBI is a Latvia based company with an easy-to-use app for powerful visualization and project analysis in Jira. Raimonds Simanovskis, CEO and founder of eazyBI, never wanted the company to be a typical startup. Even without venture capital, an office, or any high growth plans, eazyBI managed to build a company from zero to $3 million in less than seven years and create one of the most popular business intelligence apps for Jira.

### Start small; stay lean

_“I started my company all by myself in March 2011 and was working alone for about two years. I had some savings, no mortgage, I did not want to risk too much – I just wanted to explore different ideas,” says Raimonds Simanovskis._

Prior to [eazyBI](https://eazybi.com/), Raimonds worked on software development and implementation projects for more than 15 years. He had been using and implementing business intelligence products by “big vendors,” and liked the value they offered customers. However, those products were too time-consuming and delayed the insights that they could provide. This challenge lead him to come up with the idea to build an easy-to-use business intelligence application with standard data source integrations that could provide value faster. After a few initial experiments, Raimonds decided to work on it full time and founded the company eazyBI. Within a couple of months, the first beta version was launched, and half a year later the company had its first pilot customers.

Raimonds kept the company small and only decided to hire his first employee in the third year. In the fifth year, they expanded to a company of six. Now, seven years later, there are 18 people working at eazyBI and Raimonds still prefers to keep the team lean. He believes that keeping the team small allows everyone to work together more efficiently.

### Remote setup

The entire eazyBI team works 100% remotely. After working in corporate business and spending hours in traffic every day, Raimonds decided that having an office was not necessary. Why should they have an office in one location when they serve a global market? The team could work equally well or better from any place with a good internet connection. Each eazyBI employee is set up with their own “home office,” and the team is notorious for taking advantage of the summer weather and working outside. With different collaboration solutions like Slack, Trello, Zoom, and Jira, it’s easy for the team to communicate regularly while working remotely.

Additionally, the team also organizes monthly face-to-face meetings where they review business results, past and upcoming events, and can catch up with each other over a few beers. As part of those meetings, they hold internal hackathons where they brainstorm on various challenges - software, marketing, or company related.

### All-hands-on-support

At eazyBI, the team has an “all-hands-on-support” policy. This unique approach enables them to better understand the customer challenges and needs. Everyone from marketing, operations, and even their developers are involved in customer support. Every two weeks they rotate and two of them become full-time tech-support members. The approach allows the team to get direct feedback from their customers on a regular basis and find workarounds when users ask for functionality that eazyBI does not provide. This process leads to much better ideas and helps them develop useful new features, which eventually benefit all customers.

> One thing I advise to anyone is to pick an idea that you know and love to spend time on. It might take a long time to grow the business and make it profitable and even if it will fail you will not regret it if you liked what you were doing.

##### Raimonds Simanovskis

### Road to success

At first, eazyBI’s software was created as a cloud integration with other web applications (Basecamp, Highrise and later excel, google sheets, and Oracle). But Raimonds had worked with Jira for a long time in his previous company and often used reporting tools on top of Jira and saw a good business opportunity for eazyBI. Tired of the continuous paperwork that came with the direct sales, Raimonds was looking to outsource as much of the sales process as possible. The Atlassian Marketplace attracted eazyBI because it provided visibility to a large customer base, automated the sales process, and took away the pain of becoming approved vendors for customers.

In the fall of 2012, eazyBI’s cloud app for Jira was published on [Atlassian Marketplace](https://marketplace.atlassian.com/) and they started seeing early success when happy customers started purchasing the app at the end of their free trials. In the first month after their launch, eazyBI had earned $9k in-app sales—a trend that would continue netting th

##### Tempo + Atlassian

# Tempo: becoming your own company

How Tempo turned a consulting challenge into a business

---

##### Locations

Boston, MA

##### Products

[Jira Software](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira)

##### Share Page

- 
- 
- 

**Tempo products are designed to enhance Jira by helping teams collaborate, plan, budget, track, and work smarter in one central software platform. More than 11,000 customers in over 100 countries trust Tempo to be successful. Their customers include small startups to large-scale distributed enterprises such as NASA, Amazon, PayPal, Toshiba, Disney, BMW, and Princeton University. Tempo is an award-winning, best-selling app vendor in the Atlassian Marketplace and has an established network of more than 100 partners worldwide.**

Ten years ago, Pétur Ágústsson was working at an IT consulting firm in Iceland when he and his colleagues ran into a challenge. They were using Jira Software to manage projects, and a separate tool to log their hours for billing. Keeping track of which clients to bill for what work was tricky, so Pétur and his colleagues took advantage of Jira’s open platform to build an app that solved the problem. The app allowed the consultants to log their hours in the same tool they used to track work and also to generate billing reports for customers much more easily. “It was revolutionary for us,” Pétur says.

More than that, it provided them an opportunity. They soon licensed their app to another consulting company that was trying to solve the same problem, and a new business was born. A decade later, Tempo Timesheets is one of the most popular apps for Jira in the Atlassian Marketplace. Pétur is Chief Customer Officer at Tempo, which also sells Tempo Planner and Tempo Budgets. It’s a story of how an innovative team saw an opportunity in Jira’s open, extensible architecture and turned it into a business.

> It was one phone call. They said, ‘We’re a no-bullshit company, welcome to the program.’ It felt very relaxed.

##### Pétur Ágústsson

Chief Customer Officer

### An ecosystem within an ecosystem

In 2015, Tempo spun off from that IT consulting firm to become its own company with close to 100 employees and 11,000 customers for its suite of apps, which help teams better manage their projects in Jira. A further 3,000 people download the tools each month to try them out. Tempo has opened up the APIs to its own apps, allowing other developers to extend the functionality. “We’ve built an ecosystem within an ecosystem,” Pétur says.

He sees several advantages in building apps for Atlassian. It’s a fast-growing market with thousands of customers, providing Tempo with lots of opportunity for growth. Tempo is profitable and its sales are growing quickly each year, he says. In addition, Atlassian’s APIs and documentation are easy to work with, so Tempo can try out new ideas quickly.

To be successful at building apps, he recommends listening closely to customers. “Don’t assume you know what they want,” Pétur says. “Talk to users and find out what they really need.” He recommends starting with a simple app and adding new features over time.

### Building apps in the cloud era

Tempo builds apps for both the cloud and server versions of Jira. Building for Jira Cloud is quicker than building for the server version, he says, and the cloud provides more opportunity for integration with other tools. In the cloud era, he notes, customers expect more integrations out of the box.

The SaaS technology market is evolving fast, which means that developers who build apps for SaaS products need to stay nimble and pay attention to changing customer needs. “It can be hard work, but you have a great opportunity,” Pétur says.

Tempo continues to innovate and develop new ideas. It recently launched mobile versions of Tempo Planner and Tempo Timesheets, as well as a Chrome extension for Tempo Timesheets. It has also built integrations for Slack and Hipchat.

Atlassian is easy to work with, Pétur says, and he likes the company’s down-to-earth attitude. He remembers when Atlassian invited him to join the program back in 2009. “It was one phone call. They said, ‘We’re a no-bullshit company, welcome to the program.’ It felt very relaxed.”

The Atlassian Marketplace now has more than 3,000 apps and has generated over $100 million in total sales in the last fiscal year alone. Whether it’s streamlining incident management, collaborating via ChatOps, or optimizing a DevOps process, there’s an app for almost every need. Almost—because customers always want new capabilities for Atlassian products and deeper integrations with other services and tools. Identify one of those needs, and you might find yourself building the next Tempo.
